name: gpu_game_scene
title: Game scene rendering
short_description: Frame rate of a textured, lit 3D scene rendered like a game.

structured_text.description: Renders a bundled 3D scene the way a game engine does, with textured geometry, dynamic lighting, shadows and post processing, drawing as many frames as it can at a fixed resolution. The scene and settings are identical on every machine so the result depends only on the graphics processor.
structured_text.metric_explanation: The score is the average number of frames drawn per second across the run, reported in fps.
structured_text.value_interpretation: Higher is better. More frames per second means smoother play, with common targets around 60 and above for a fluid experience.
structured_text.reasoning: A full game style scene exercises the whole graphics pipeline together, which makes it the most direct measure of gaming performance.

domain_gaming.explanation: This is the defining workload for gaming performance.

domain_office.explanation: Office machines do not run game scenes.

domain_software_development.explanation: Game and engine developers test against exactly this kind of scene.

domain_graphics_design.explanation: Real time preview of 3D assets shares the same rendering path.

domain_video_editing.explanation: Real time graphics overlays and motion previews reuse engine rendering.

domain_3d_rendering.explanation: Real time viewports and game ready content depend on this pipeline.

domain_audio_production.explanation: Audio work has no real time 3D rendering.

domain_cad_engineering.explanation: Interactive design review uses similar real time rendering.

domain_scientific_computing.explanation: Real time visualisation borrows from this rendering style.

domain_ai_training.explanation: Simulated environments for training render scenes like this.

domain_ai_inference.explanation: Inference rarely drives real time rendering.

domain_data_science.explanation: Analytics does not depend on game rendering.

domain_server_virt.explanation: Cloud gaming and streaming servers render exactly these scenes remotely.
